growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: TrendForce now sees 2Q26 conventional DRAM pricing up 58-63% QoQ as supply is reallocated to AI servers, while Micron has already put HBM4 and Gen6 AI storage into volume production for NVIDIA Vera Rubin, giving it direct leverage to both the HBM mix shift and the broader memory upcycle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron is still in the sweet spot of the AI memory cycle: its March 18, 2026 earnings release showed record results and very strong fiscal Q3 guidance, while its March 16, 2026 HBM4 and Gen6 AI storage launch confirms product leadership tied to NVIDIA's next platform ramp.
Microsoft combines top-tier quality and cash generation with a 33% drawdown, strong Azure/AI demand, and near-term enterprise AI monetization catalysts, though heavy capex keeps it below STRONG BUY.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Chair and CEO William McDermott bought 28,682 ServiceNow shares for about $3.0M on February 27, 2026 in direct open-market purchases, a rare large-cap CEO cash buy that increased his personal stake and was disclosed in a Form 4 filed March 2, 2026.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rare CEO open-market buying, strong 21% subscription growth with expanding AI partnerships, and near-term earnings/event catalysts make ServiceNow a credible large-cap AI workflow rebound buy after a 52% drawdown.

catalysts_general: Discovery: DECK stands out as a profitable brand owner that remains deeply discounted versus its 2025 highs despite HOKA and UGG still driving beats, raised guidance, and large buybacks.
growth_picks.analyst_take: DECK is a profitable premium-footwear compounder trading at a reasonable multiple while HOKA and UGG still post growth, buybacks remain aggressive, and the next earnings print can confirm the reset is overdone.
Cheap on forward earnings and highly cash generative, with accelerating B2B momentum and a near-term earnings catalyst, though competitive search and AI risks cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Pershing Square disclosed a brand-new 2,673,569-share Meta position worth $1.7648B in its Q4 2025 13F, instantly making it an 11.37% portfolio position and one of Bill Ackman's biggest reported bets.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Meta remains a high-quality ad and AI platform trading well below its 52-week high, with late-April earnings as a catalyst, but the 2026 capex surge and regulatory overhang cap conviction.

Mattel screens as a cheap cash-generative toy/IP name with a CEO open-market buy, a large new repurchase authorization, and multiple 2026 catalysts, though tariff exposure and soft North America demand keep conviction below high.

Adobe is a contrarian large-cap software buy near its 52-week low because record Q1 FY2026 results, tripling AI-first ARR and strong cash flow are not fully reflected in a single-digit forward earnings multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Roughly 40% below its 52-week high and still near the lows, QCOM is a profitable picks-and-shovels name with a $20B buyback authorization and an April 29, 2026 earnings catalyst that can show whether handset weakness tied to AI-memory shortages is stabilizing; the bear case is that handset softness lasts longer than the market expects.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Qualcomm is a high-cash-flow semiconductor franchise trading near its 52-week low at roughly 11x forward earnings, with a large new buyback and an April 29 earnings reset that could re-rate the stock if handset constraints start to ease.
Cheap, cash-generative consumer cyber platform with raised FY26 guidance and debt-maturity relief, but leverage and MoneyLion-related legal risk keep it below high conviction.

SoFi still has a credible upside setup after a 51% drawdown because member and revenue growth remain strong, the business is shifting toward more fee-based loan platform revenue, and CEO Anthony Noto has been buying stock, but credit/accounting scrutiny and very high beta keep conviction below high.
FDA approval of AVLAYAH de-risks Denali's lead asset and validates the blood-brain-barrier platform, but commercialization and confirmatory-trial risk keep this out of strong-buy territory.
GPN screens as a cash-generative payments value play after closing Worldpay, with buybacks and integration-driven EPS upside offset by leverage and execution risk.

FIS looks like a contrarian value buy because the stock is still near its lows while the company remains highly cash-generative, but the upside depends on clean Total Issuing integration and steady deleveraging after the January 2026 reshuffle.

FDA approval for acquired hypothalamic obesity and a likely Q2 2026 EU label expansion materially widen IMCIVREE's commercial runway, but the recent EMANATE miss and ongoing cash burn keep conviction below high.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: TTD became one of the highest-signal insider names on the board after founder-CEO Jeff Green personally bought about $148M of stock into a brutal drawdown.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Massive CEO insider buying and high-margin cash generation make the drawdown interesting, but the Publicis audit fallout creates unresolved client-retention risk that keeps this at hold pending the next earnings update.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Down about 44% from its 52-week high with the stock still only around 15% above the low, CPNG has a clean contrarian setup because Greenoaks-linked director Neil Mehta bought roughly $137M into the data-breach panic and the next hard catalyst is May 5, 2026 earnings, though the bear case is that breach-related customer damage and regulatory fallout linger longer than bulls think.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Post-breach customer trends appear to be stabilizing and a major insider bought aggressively, but unresolved Korean regulatory fallout, thin operating margins, and a still-demanding multiple keep Coupang from clearing a BUY bar.

CROX is optically cheap and cash generative, but the stock still needs proof that HEYDUDE can stabilize and that 2026 tariff pressure will not eat the margin recovery before the valuation discount closes.

catalysts_general: Discovery: ALGN looks like a washed-out quality compounder because the stock is still far below prior highs even as record Invisalign volumes and renewed buybacks suggest the core franchise is stabilizing.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality clear-aligner leader is stabilizing with record Q4 volume, a large remaining buyback, and an April 29 earnings catalyst, but modest 2026 growth, tariff exposure, and no bullish insider signal keep it below BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's March 26, 2026 annual report showed Europe and Canada lifted defense spending 20% year over year, which should keep air and missile defense budgets flowing toward Patriot, ESSM and related RTX systems already embedded with Germany and other NATO customers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has credible defense and aerospace demand support, but at roughly $198 the stock is near highs, carries a rich forward multiple, and offers limited upside relative to its risk profile.

catalysts_general: Discovery: U.S. semiconductor policy is shifting from fabs to full domestic supply-chain depth, and Amkor is a direct beneficiary because Arizona packaging and test capacity is the missing geopolitical bottleneck for turning TSMC's U.S. wafers into truly onshore chips.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI and U.S. packaging catalysts are real, but the stock is not cheap enough to offset the 2026 capex surge and recent selling overhang.

catalysts_general: Discovery: TNK fits the same rotation because its spot-market exposure gives it direct leverage to stronger tanker rates at a moment when energy shipping constraints are back in focus.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TNK is a cheap, debt-free tanker operator with solid spot-rate support into Q1 2026, but the shares are already near 52-week highs and the upside is constrained by shipping-rate cyclicality.

catalysts_general: Discovery: RKLB qualifies as a fresh news-driven opportunity because the company keeps stacking contract and backlog wins that reinforce its shift from niche launcher to scaled defense-space platform.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rocket Lab is stacking real defense and space-systems wins, but the stock still looks too dependent on flawless Neutron execution after management pushed first launch to Q4 2026 and opened a fresh up-to-$1B equity distribution in March 2026.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The April 2, 2026 tightening of Section 232 tariffs on steel, aluminum and copper derivatives should improve the domestic pricing umbrella for Nucor, whose earnings remain highly sensitive to import pressure and U.S. trade enforcement.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nucor has real near-term support from stronger Q1 pricing/volumes and tariff protection, but with the stock only about 12% below its high, negative trailing free cash flow, and no meaningful recent insider buying, the setup looks like a quality cyclical hold rather than a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Dan Loeb's Third Point increased Union Pacific by 935,000 shares in Q4 2025, lifting the stake 106.86% to 1.81M shares worth $418.69M, which is a meaningful size-up in an efficiency-sensitive industrial compounder.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Union Pacific is a high-quality railroad still executing well on pricing, service, and cash generation, but the stock is not cheap enough to offset muted freight demand and merger-regulatory risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Applied Digital's March 30 lease amendments added a CoreWeave guaranty and $50 million letter-of-credit support, materially de-risking cash flows for one of the cleaner public ways to play AI data-hall and power-capacity scarcity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI data-center lease momentum is real and recent credit enhancements help, but extreme financing dependence, negative free cash flow, and concentrated customer risk make the stock too execution-sensitive to chase ahead of earnings.

catalysts_general: Discovery: TVTX remains a clean biotech event trade because FILSPARI's FSGS decision is due April 13, 2026 and approval would open the first approved therapy in that indication.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Strong FILSPARI commercial traction in IgAN and a near-term April 13 FSGS FDA decision create upside, but the setup remains too binary after DUPLEX missed its primary eGFR endpoint and the FDA extended review for more clinical-benefit detail.

Diamondback is a high-quality Permian operator with strong 2025 free cash flow, buybacks, and near-term earnings catalysts, but the stock is near its 52-week high and recent insider/SGF selling leaves limited margin of safety.

SSRM is a fundamentally improved gold miner with a de-risking Copler sale, positive free cash flow, and a live buyback, but the stock is already near its 52-week high and still carries Turkey closing and cost-risk overhangs.
Coty is statistically cheap and has strategic optionality after debt reduction, but pulled FY26 guidance and a weak Q3 outlook keep it in prove-it mode.
Alphabet is a high-quality compounder with accelerating Cloud/AI demand and growing Waymo optionality, but the stock is not cheap enough to offset antitrust and capex pressure at current levels.
Nebius has real hyperscaler-backed AI cloud demand, but at roughly 17% below its 52-week high the stock still looks too expensive relative to its heavy cash burn, capex load, and financing risk.

Snap is a credible turnaround candidate with improving ad execution, positive free cash flow, a buyback, and an activist catalyst, but weak near-term guidance, persistent competitive pressure, and ongoing insider selling keep it below BUY quality.

Integrated terminal and fuel-retail assets support solid cash flow and an increasing distribution, but limited upside, very high leverage, and volatile wholesale margins keep GLP as an income hold rather than a fresh buy.

McCormick's base business is stable after a Q1 beat and reaffirmed 2026 outlook, but the planned Unilever Foods combination adds dilution, leverage, and antitrust overhang that weakens the near-term risk/reward.

Frontline has a real near-term earnings catalyst from high-rate VLCC charters, vessel-sale gains, and fleet renewal, but shipping remains deeply cyclical and the stock is already trading close to its 52-week high with leverage still elevated.

EchoStar is still a balance-sheet rescue story rather than a value story: the AT&T spectrum sale can ease the 2026 debt wall, but the stock is already near its highs despite negative free cash flow, explicit going-concern language, heavy leverage, and limited upside if the rescue closes.

Interlink has interesting niche sensor design wins, but worsening margins, continuing losses, tiny institutional sponsorship, and limited insider conviction make the 70% drawdown look more like a value trap than an entry point.
